Event: Daniels tells Carver his sergeant rank was not earned and that Greggs will have authority over him
Explanation: Clarifies chain of command and standards, slightly hurting ego but guiding Carver’s development and helping the unit.

Event: Carver begins to tire of doing lowly surveillance jobs despite being promoted to Sergeant
Explanation: His growing frustration harms his own morale without clearly affecting others.

Event: Carver initially tries to defend Daniels and the assignment to an irate Herc
Explanation: He maintains discipline and backs his superior, modestly helping his own standing and the unit’s cohesion.

Event: Unit fails to notify Carver and Herc that Nick Sobotka has turned himself in
Explanation: Poor communication wastes their time and harms them without benefitting anyone.

Event: Carver hits his rage breaking point over the wasted stakeout time
Explanation: His anger mainly damages his own composure and judgment, with little benefit to others.

Event: Carver declares he will transfer to an open Sergeant’s post in Colvin’s district
Explanation: Moving to a post that recognizes his rank improves his prospects and puts his skills where they’re better used.

Event: Carver takes Herc with him to Colvin’s district
Explanation: He secures a better position for himself and improves Herc’s situation by bringing him along.

Event: Carver procures Marlo’s number (via Herc) and presents it to an impressed Lester
Explanation: He gains credit and advances the investigation, benefiting both himself and the case.

Event: Herc actually obtains Marlo’s number and passes it to Carver
Explanation: Herc’s work helps the investigation and boosts Carver, while also reflecting well on himself.

Event: Carver officially reports Colicchio for excessive force
Explanation: He protects his career and integrity by disciplining brutality, which helps the system but directly harms Colicchio’s interests.

Event: Carver is forced by rank to write up Anthony Colicchio for police brutality
Explanation: Complying with duty safeguards his own position and institutional legitimacy while penalizing Colicchio.

Event: Carver serves as Burrell’s mole inside Daniels’ detail in Season 1
Explanation: He gains promotion and favor at the cost of undermining his unit’s trust and effectiveness.

Event: Burrell rewards Carver with promotion to Sergeant for being his mole
Explanation: Burrell secures an ally and extends influence while promoting someone for political reasons over merit.

Event: Carver scores worse than Herc on the Sergeant’s exam yet is still promoted
Explanation: He benefits from an unearned promotion that disadvantages a better-scoring colleague.

Event: Carver later earns genuine respect and authority by showing leadership skills
Explanation: He develops real competence and leadership, substantially helping both his career and his team.

Event: Carver is promoted to Lieutenant on merit at the end of the series
Explanation: He receives a deserved advancement that rewards performance and likely improves command quality.

Event: Carver follows Colvin’s advice and becomes a reference figure in the neighborhood
Explanation: Adopting community-oriented policing boosts his effectiveness and greatly benefits residents.

Event: Carver takes lessons from Daniels and Colvin to heart and becomes a respected leader
Explanation: Internalizing mentorship improves his own trajectory and the welfare and performance of his men.

Event: Carver evolves from a hardass cop who barely acknowledges the community to caring about it
Explanation: His shift toward empathy aids his own professionalism and deeply benefits the people he polices.
